/trust-2.title: Surya Raj
h1: Reducing Drop-off With Trust-First UX Patterns
Small UI components that subtlety say: “Hey, you’re safe here”
h2: If users are thinking, “Wait, is this safe?” — you’ve already lost them.
Retirement is a long game — and we were the new player.
You’re asking for their money and personal info — trust isn’t optional.
We knew trust couldn’t be asked for — it had to be designed in.
Here’s what actually made the difference:
The biggest takeaway?
h3: As a new player in the retirement space, user trust was everything. When it comes to money, people are extra careful — and ghxghcjchjch so. safasfSo in this project, we worked on designing small but powerful trust elements — we call them trust boxes.

When you're a new brand dealing with something as serious as money and long journeys like retirement, users hesitate.     They don’t know who you are yet, and you’re suddenly asking for sensitive details like PAN, Aadhaar, bank info — it’s a lot.
People are sharing their money, personal details, and financial future. If they don’t trust the app, they won’t use it
Instead of shouting “Trust us!”, we designed quiet signals throughout the journey
We built small, clear trust messages and badges throughout the app to make users feel safe and confident at every step.We created two types of Trust Boxes:
Trust made a difference, Users dropped off less and moved through sensitive steps more confidently.
h4: >> UX Strategy – Our Trust Design Approach

/b2b-dashboard.title: Surya Raj
h1: Making Retirement Simple for Everyone
Making Tax-Saving Seamless for Companies and Employees
h2: Making Retirement Planning Simple for Companies
The Gap in Employee Wellbeing
What Users - HR & Employees Really Wanted
What Users Really Wanted”
Here’s what actually made the difference:
Employee Mobile View
After Launch: What Changed
Designed for Everyone - Not all HR users are tech-savvy,
h3: At PensionBox, we help companies take care of their employees’ retirement savings. Corporate NPS programs were messy — HR had to deal with spreadsheets, emails, and follow-ups, and employees often didn’t know their contribution status.

I designed a dashboard that makes it easy for HR to manage everything in one place and for employees to see their progress clearly. The goal was simple: make the process smooth, clear, and stress-free for everyone.

HR teams faced a lot of friction: onboarding employees, setting up contribution rules, and processing payroll was slow and error-prone. Employees often didn’t know where they stood or how much the company was contributing.
The opportunity was clear: a single dashboard that could simplify HR workflows, increase transparency, and boost employee trust and participation.

We didn’t conduct formal research, but over the years, our team had collected insights from sales meetings, client requests, and meetups. We understood HR pain points and employee expectations deeply:
Easy onboarding for employees
Clear contribution tracking
Fast, error-free payroll processing
Transparent reporting and summaries
These requirements shaped every part of the dashboard.
The answers were blunt and golden:
Employees wanted to save taxes while contributing.
Key pain points from HR and employees.
Dashboard (HR):HR gets a clear overview of participation rates, pending approvals, and contribution summaries — all in one place.
Add Employees:Easily onboard new employees and manage their participation in the NPS program without juggling spreadsheets.
Rollout:Set up contribution plans and schedules for the whole company in just a few clicks.
Payroll:Integrate contributions seamlessly with payroll to automate deductions and updates.
Edge Cases & EnhancementsHandle exceptions like late enrollments, withdrawals, or contribution changes smoothly, with clear notifications.
Employees can see their contributions, track progress, and make changes if allowed — all from their phone.
Drop-offs at payment and FATCA stages reduced significantly
Users completed onboarding in fewer attempts
People mentioned the process felt “safe and understandable”
Internal support tickets for NPS issues went down
So we had to design the dashboard that should work for both first-time and advanced HR users, balancing simplicity with full control.
p: Download match data or initiate link.

/nps.title: Surya Raj
h1: UX That Simplifies Retirement Planning
Making NPS jargon - FATCA, PRAN, Tier 2 Account feel like a friendly word.
h2: “Wait... What is NPS?”
What users were struggling with
Before designing, we needed to listen
We needed a flow that guides, not confuses
Here’s what actually made the difference:
15+ Micro Fixes That Make Everything Feel Smoother
After Launch: What Changed
The biggest takeaway? Small things—like a tooltip or trust badge—can have massive impact when placed with intent.
h3: That’s the vibe we got from most people when we first started working on this flow.At PensionBox, we wanted to help users plan for retirement in a simple, confident way. But the process was full of government terms, long forms, and confusing steps.So our goal was simple:
“Let’s make it feel easy, safe, and quick — even for someone doing it for the first time.”

You get dropped into 10+ form fields, redirected to random partner sites (hi, KFintech 👋), and are told to enter things like “FATCA declaration” without any clue what that even is.So before doing anything design-wise, we decided to talk to people.
We grouped users into 3 levels:
Level 0 — Never heard of NPS
Level 1 — Aware but never invested
Level 2 — Actively using NPS through Groww, KFintech, etc.
The answers were blunt and golden:
“I didn’t even know I had to declare anything.”
“I got scared and just closed the tab.”

“Too many steps. And I didn’t know which one was safe.”Once we understood our users and mapped the problems, we needed to visually structure the journey a user would take to open an NPS account inside PensionBox.

Step-by-step onboarding:The old onboarding screens showed long forms with 10+ fields at once, no progress indicator, and no sense of direction. Users didn’t know how many steps were left, or if they were even doing it right.

Trust Boxes & Friendly UX Writing:Users were getting nervous when asked to enter personal data like PAN, Aadhaar, or make payments — especially when redirected to unfamiliar domains like KFintech.Government-style language like “Proceed to FATCA Declaration” or “Generate PRAN” was confusing and intimidating.
Smart Form Design:Users were abandoning forms because they were long, not mobile-friendly, or gave no validation feedback.
Save & Resume Later:NPS onboarding takes time. Some users dropped off mid-way and had to start over — frustrating experience.
The launch wasn't the end — it was just phase one. We continuously improved the experience based on real user feedback, friction logs, and observed drop-offs.
Drop-offs at payment and FATCA stages reduced significantly
Users completed onboarding in fewer attempts
People mentioned the process felt “safe and understandable”
Internal support tickets for NPS issues went down
This project taught me how tiny trust cues can make or break a complex flow — and how simplicity is often hidden behind hundreds of design decisions.
p: Download match data or initiate link.

/modue.title: Surya Raj
h1: 3D Visuals for Custom Control Interfaces
h2: Modue is a modular control system designed to elevate workflows with precision and innovation, with full control over your computer station, customizable widgets, and seamless integration to maximize efficiency - it offers perfect control at your fingertipsThey reached out to me to create a scroll-based animation that showcases their products in an engaging and interactive way
Understood their product architecture, use cases, and the tone they wanted to projectDefined the key product features they wanted highlighted during the scroll (modularity, control, customization)
Created low-fidelity motion storyboards showing how the product modules would animate as users scrollDecided key “trigger points” for animation states (enter, focus, swap, expand)

Modeled and rendered the control modules using Blender  Designed smooth transitions that reflected the real-world feel of toggling hardware  Incorporated light, texture, and depth to make the hardware feel premium and tactile
Converted 3D renders into lightweight, web-optimized Lottie files for seamless integration
Designed additional frames and alternate details as part of the creative process, offering Modue more options to explore:
h3: Overview

Modue is a modular control system designed to elevate workflows with precision and innovation, with full control over your computer station, customizable widgets, and seamless integration to maximize efficiency - it offers perfect control at your fingertipsThey reached out to me to create a scroll-based animation that showcases their products in an engaging and interactive way
Discovery & Requirements
Understood their product architecture, use cases, and the tone they wanted to projectDefined the key product features they wanted highlighted during the scroll (modularity, control, customization)
Storyboard
Created low-fidelity motion storyboards showing how the product modules would animate as users scrollDecided key “trigger points” for animation states (enter, focus, swap, expand)
3D Modeling & Animation

Modeled and rendered the control modules using Blender  Designed smooth transitions that reflected the real-world feel of toggling hardware  Incorporated light, texture, and depth to make the hardware feel premium and tactile
Final Animation
Converted 3D renders into lightweight, web-optimized Lottie files for seamless integration
Frames and Details
Designed additional frames and alternate details as part of the creative process, offering Modue more options to explore:
